subject: Ecology
title: Dung Beetles
description: A short lesson to help you apply Dung Beetles.

hook.text: Dung beetles bury animal waste underground, and entire grasslands depend on them for it.

buildup.text: By burying dung, they recycle nutrients, aerate soil, and reduce parasites on the surface.

discovery.text: Australia imported African dung beetles because native species couldn't process cattle dung.

twist.text: Without dung beetles, pastures in Australia were being buried under undecomposed cow pats.

climax.text: Dung beetles save the global cattle industry an estimated $380 million a year in soil services.

punchline.text: The most underrated farmer works with dung.

quiz.question: Why did Australia import African dung beetles?
options: Native beetles couldn't process cattle dung
